title: Munshot — Concall Deep Dive Report
description: The single contract for one company's latest-quarter earnings-call research report. Every later build step (renderer, editable model, valuation, verdict) keys off this shape. One run = one company. Facts carry a `source` wherever the schema allows: 'Transcript' | 'PPT' | 'Web' | 'Est.'.

meta.description: Run identity, price inputs, and source links.

slug.description: URL-safe id used as the KV key and the /api/report?slug= param.

quarter_confirmed.description: True when the quarter was verified against the transcript/PPT, not inferred.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the pipeline finished writing this report.

transcript_available.description: Whether a concall transcript was found. Downstream `source: 'Transcript'` facts require this to be true.

sources.description: Source links. All three keys must be present so link rendering has stable fields to read; use null when a link/date is unavailable.

transcript_url.description: Link to the concall transcript, or null.

ppt_url.description: Link to the investor presentation, or null.

concall_date.description: Date the earnings call was held, or null.

web.description: Optional list of web sources cited by the report (for the Sources panel). Each entry is the page a Web-sourced fact links to.

inputs.description: Price/size inputs the valuation math must reconcile against. market_cap_cr = cmp * shares_out_cr; EV = market_cap_cr + net_debt_cr. All are required — valuation (P/E, EV/EBITDA, P/S) cannot be computed without them.

net_debt_cr.description: Net debt (negative if net cash) in INR crore. null when Screener doesn't surface it (self-check treats it as non-fatal); the valuation math then treats EV net debt as 0 and the UI/exports show '—'.

key_takeaways.description: 5-7 decision-relevant bullets. The tl;dr an analyst reads first.

about.description: Business overview — what the company does and how revenue/margin split.

segment_reported.description: True when the company reports segment splits; false when the mix below is estimated.

pct.description: Share of revenue, percent (0-100). Should sum to ~100 across segments.

ebitda_margin.description: Segment EBITDA margin, percent. null when the company does not disclose the segment's margin (the renderer shows 'not disclosed'; never invent a number).

concall.description: Structured read of the earnings call itself.

metric.description: What is being guided, e.g. 'Revenue growth'.

statement.description: Verbatim-ish paraphrase of what management said.

value.description: Extracted number/range if hard, else null.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F in the linked document. Optional; null when no clean verbatim quote exists.

source_url.description: Specific source URL for this fact (mainly Web facts; Transcript/PPT resolve to meta.sources by source category). Optional.

themes.description: Recurring topics on the call with a stance and evidence.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F in the linked document. Optional; null when no clean verbatim quote exists.

source_url.description: Specific source URL for this fact (mainly Web facts; Transcript/PPT resolve to meta.sources by source category). Optional.

tone_shift_vs_last_quarter.description: How management's tone/framing moved versus the prior call.

expansion_flags.description: Metrics that moved materially (capacity, orderbook, capex, headcount…).

thesis_triggers.description: Pre-defined checks: did the call confirm the bull-case triggers?

quote.description: Verbatim span from the transcript backing this trigger, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F. Optional; null when no clean quote exists.

classification.description: Tags that place the quarter in context (e.g. 'Beat', 'Guidance raise', 'One-off').

risks.description: Risks, typically corroborated from the web rather than the call.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F in the linked document. Optional; null when no clean verbatim quote exists.

source_url.description: Specific source URL for this risk (Web facts carry the citation URL). Optional.

management_tone.description: Per-theme read on how confident management sounded.

anchor.description: The phrase/exchange that anchors this read.

analyst_tone.description: How the sell-side analysts on the call behaved.

thesis.description: Bull case. Each point pairs with the observation that would falsify it.

falsifier.description: What we'd have to see to conclude this point is wrong.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F in the linked document. Optional; null when no clean verbatim quote exists.

source_url.description: Specific source URL for this point (mainly Web facts; Transcript/PPT resolve to meta.sources by source category). Optional.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source, copied word-for-word so it is findable via Ctrl+F in the linked document. Optional; null when no clean verbatim quote exists.

source_url.description: Specific source URL for this point (mainly Web facts; Transcript/PPT resolve to meta.sources by source category). Optional.

financials.description: The editable model. `rows` is the P&L skeleton; `assumptions` are the levers step-5 will make editable.

rows.description: One object per metric. Convention (in order): Revenue, Gross margin %, EBITDA, EBITDA margin %, Adjusted EBITDA margin % (optional), PAT, Net margin %. Absolute rows carry unit 'rs_cr' (INR crore); margin rows carry unit 'pct'. Downstream code keys off the stable `key`, never the display `metric`. The six non-optional keys MUST each be present (adj_ebitda_margin_pct is the only optional row) so valuation/renderer code never hits a missing key. `uniqueItems` blocks exact-duplicate rows; the pipeline additionally enforces one row per key.

key.description: Stable machine id. Step-5's valuation recompute derives EV/EBITDA, P/S and P/E from these keys deterministically — never by string-matching the display label. `adj_ebitda_margin_pct` is the one optional row.

driver.description: One line on what moves this row.

basis.description: What the growth assumption rests on.

note.description: Caveats — what could break the model.

valuation.description: Multiples implied by meta.inputs + financials. Must be internally consistent: PE = cmp / (PAT/shares_out_cr); EV/EBITDA = (market_cap_cr + net_debt_cr) / EBITDA; P/S = market_cap_cr / Revenue.

fy27e.description: null = 'n.m.' (denominator ≤ 0, e.g. loss-making / negative EBITDA); the UI and exports render null as 'n.m.'.
null = 'n.m.' (denominator ≤ 0, e.g. loss-making / negative EBITDA); the UI and exports render null as 'n.m.'.
null = 'n.m.' (denominator ≤ 0, e.g. loss-making / negative EBITDA); the UI and exports render null as 'n.m.'.

sanity_check.description: One paragraph reconciling the multiples to the story — is the price already pricing in the guidance?

next_steps.description: The verdict and what to watch.

rerating_triggers.description: Events that would move the multiple.
